text: Erich Clar published his aromatic $\pi$-sextet rule in 1972, giving a very useful qualitative tool for deciding the degree of aromaticity of different types of benzenoid compounds. It has endured for the past 40 years and still remains a focal point for interest within the chemical community having been supported by vast amounts of experimental and computational evidence. In this review we provide an overview of the number of examples, which continually demonstrate the relevance of the $\pi$-sextet rule to aromaticity. Lastly, a couple of more recent examples of their use in more current research is discussed.
